summary: Route auto-mode search corpus-first: when the local store holds at least one archived post, search the corpus; the live publication-directory endpoint serves --data-source live and auto with no corpus. Local provenance reads freshness from the 'posts' sync key that archive actually stamps.
reason: The API's only live search endpoint, GET /api/v1/publication/search on substack.com, searches the publication DIRECTORY (find newsletters by name or topic), not post content, and answers [] for content queries. The generated live-first auto routing therefore shadowed a populated local corpus with empty live results, so 'search them offline' returned zero hits right after a successful archive. Corpus presence is decided by an actual posts row, not by data.db existing, so a schema-only database cannot hijack auto routing away from live. The previous provenance lookup read a 'search' sync key that no command writes, reporting unknown freshness even immediately after archive.
validated_outcome: With an 89-post corpus, plain `search "streaming" --agent` returns 50 dated hits with meta {source: local, reason: corpus_first} and a real synced_at; with a schema-only database or no database, auto mode still reaches the live directory endpoint; --data-source live and --data-source local behave unchanged.

deferred_to_upstream.feature: A spec-level way to mark a live search endpoint as a DIRECTORY search (entity discovery) rather than content search, so generated auto-mode routing prefers a populated local corpus without a hand patch.
deferred_to_upstream.reason: The generated search template assumes the API's search endpoint answers the same question as local FTS; on per-publication content APIs that assumption inverts the correct default.
